back_translation: And Balaam said to Balak: 'Build for me here seven altars, and prepare for me here seven bulls and seven rams.'
And Balak did as Balaam had spoken; and Balak and Balaam offered a bull and a ram on the altar.
And Balaam said to Balak: 'Stand by your burnt offering, and I will go; perhaps the LORD will come to meet me, and the word — what He shows me — I will tell you.' And he went to a barren height.
And God met with Balaam, and he said to Him: 'I have set up the seven altars, and offered a bull and a ram on the altar.'
And the LORD put a word in Balaam's mouth, and said: 'Return to Balak, and thus shall you speak.'
And he returned to him; and behold, he was standing by his burnt offering, he and all the princes of Moab.
And he lifted up his oracle and said: 'From Aram Balak has brought me, the king of Moab, from the mountains of the east: Come, curse Jacob for me; and come, denounce Israel.'
'How shall I curse, whom God has not cursed? And how shall I denounce, whom the LORD has not denounced?'
'For from the top of the cliffs I see him, and from the hills I behold him; behold, a people dwelling alone, and not reckoning himself among the nations.'
'Who has counted the dust of Jacob, or the number of even a fourth of Israel? Let my soul die the death of the upright, and let my end be like his.'
And Balak said to Balaam: 'What have you done to me? To curse my enemies I took you, and behold, you have blessed indeed.'
And he answered and said: 'Should I not what the LORD puts in my mouth — that I take heed to speak?'
And Balak said to him: 'Please come with me to another place, from where you shall see him; only the edge of him you shall see, and all of him you shall not see; and curse him for me from there.'
And he took him to the field of Zophim, to the top of Pisgah, and built seven altars, and offered a bull and a ram on the altar.
And he said to Balak: 'Stand here by your burnt offering, and I will meet thus over there.'
And the LORD met with Balaam, and put a word in his mouth, and said: 'Return to Balak, and thus shall you speak.'
And he came to him; and behold, he was standing by his burnt offering, and the princes of Moab with him; and Balak said to him: 'What did the LORD speak?'
And he lifted up his oracle and said: 'Arise, Balak, and hear; give ear to me, son of Zippor.'
'God is not a man, that He should lie, nor a son of man, that He should change His mind. Has He said, and will not do? And spoken, and will not establish it?'
'Behold, to bless I have received; and He has blessed, and I will not turn it back.'
'He has not looked on iniquity in Jacob, and has not seen trouble in Israel; the LORD his God is with him, and the shout of a King is among him.'
'God who brought them out from Egypt — like the horns of a wild ox to him.'
'For there is no enchantment in Jacob, and no divination in Israel; at this time it shall be said of Jacob and of Israel: What has God done!'
'Behold, a people rises like a lioness, and like a lion he lifts himself up; he shall not lie down until he eats prey, and drinks the blood of the slain.'
And Balak said to Balaam: 'Also curse — do not curse him at all; also bless — do not bless him at all.'
And Balaam answered and said to Balak: 'Did I not speak to you saying: All that the LORD speaks — that I will do?'
And Balak said to Balaam: 'Please come, I will take you to another place; perhaps it will be right in the eyes of God, and you will curse him for me from there.'
And Balak took Balaam to the top of Peor, which looks down over the face of the wasteland.
And Balaam said to Balak: 'Build for me here seven altars, and prepare for me here seven bulls and seven rams.'
And Balak did as Balaam had said; and he offered a bull and a ram on the altar.
